main_ingredients: spices (cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, chili, garam masala)

serving.description: 1 portion (approximately 300 g including bread and accompaniments)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken is approximately 300 g, comprising grilled spiced chicken pieces wrapped in lavash flatbread and served with tomato sauce, onion salad, and fresh herbs.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, roasted (USDA SR Legacy #05013) combined with USDA Foundation Foods grilled chicken and spice marinade component estimates
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken is not present as a single entry in any primary regional database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken (FDC #171477, cooked chicken breast/thigh blend) as the primary protein-fat-energy anchor, with carbohydrate and sodium adjusted upward to account for the yogurt-spice marinade and lavash flatbread component typical of a restaurant Beyti Kebab portion. Values represent a blended per-100g estimate for the composite dish (chicken + marinade + bread component weighted at approximately 60% chicken, 15% marinade, 25% flatbread). This is an estimated composite; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken as a composite dish. GI estimated at 47 (low) based on analogous mixed meat-and-flatbread kebab dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables. The flatbread (lavash) component contributes the primary glycemic load; yogurt marinade and protein-rich chicken lower the overall GI relative to plain bread. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 42.0) / 100 = 19.7, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 14.0 × 3 = 42.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from marinade spices, flatbread, and restaurant preparation
hypertension.guidance: A 300 g restaurant serving provides approximately 1260 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from flatbread component offset by high protein and low GI marinade
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 47 and a glycemic load of ~19.7 per serving, Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, particularly the flatbread component, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from grilled chicken, yogurt marinade, and flatbread
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish provides approximately 5.7 g saturated fat and 1260 mg sodium per serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and choose skinless chicken preparations with reduced-sodium marinades to align with AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken is a Turkish-origin grilled kebab dish in which chicken is marinated in a yogurt-based tandoori spice blend — typically including cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, chili, garam masala, garlic, ginger, and lemon juice — then grilled or roasted at high heat to produce charred, aromatic, tender meat. The dish takes its name from the Beyti restaurant in Istanbul, where the original Beyti Kebab was created using minced lamb or beef; this variant substitutes tandoori-marinated chicken, reflecting the fusion of Turkish kebab tradition with South Asian tandoor cooking techniques. The chicken is typically served wrapped in lavash or thin flatbread, accompanied by tomato sauce, grilled tomatoes, onion salad, fresh parsley, and mint. The result is a protein-rich, moderately caloric dish with a complex spice profile — smoky, tangy, mildly spicy, and aromatic. Nutritionally, a 300 g serving provides approximately 58.5 g protein, 21.6 g fat, and 42 g carbohydrate, with notable niacin, vitamin B6, phosphorus, and selenium content from the chicken. The sodium content is elevated due to the spice marinade and flatbread, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low), with a moderate glycemic load of approximately 19.7 per serving, driven primarily by the flatbread component. In Singapore, this dish is served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace at Boat Quay and is popular as a hearty main course.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Beyti Kebab tandoori chicken?
What is the difference between Beyti Kebab and regular kebab?
What spices are in tandoori chicken kebab?
Is Beyti Kebab good for diabetics?
How much protein in Beyti Kebab?
What is the glycemic index of kebab with flatbread?
Where can I get Beyti Kebab in Singapore?

confidence_reason: Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken is not present as a single composite entry in any primary regional food composition database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, USDA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken as the primary anchor with estimated adjustments for the marinade and flatbread components. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed meat-flatbread kebab dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the chicken component is well-characterised and the flatbread adjustment is based on established GI data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates for the full dish (chicken + yogurt-spice marinade + lavash flatbread) weighted approximately 60% chicken, 15% marinade, 25% flatbread per 100 g of the served dish. Direct laboratory analysis of this specific restaurant preparation would be required for high-confidence values. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category and is not reliably estimated for the composite dish. 3. GI of 47 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the accessed date. 4. The dish is served at Sultan Palace (Boat Quay) on field survey data at SGD 31.90. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant's Turkish-Lebanese cuisine profile; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.1 reflects a trace amount from the grilled chicken and marinade; this is a plausible estimate consistent with USDA data for grilled poultry.
